channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: There is a ‘renaissance’ in San Francisco, NBA commissioner says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- For the past few days, San Francisco was the center of the basketball universe. It was the first time in 25 years NBA All-Star weekend was in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Northwestern golfers invited to Augusta National Women's Amateur
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. - Northwestern is going to be well-represented at the 6th Annual Augusta National Women's Amateur Championship. Lauryn Nguyen and Elise Lee are set to the make the trip to Georgia along with NU commit Arianna Lau. "It's such a special place," noted Nguyen.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The winning total last year: 211 points. The winning total this year: 41 points. An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ludvig Aberg rallies down the stretch at Torrey Pines to win the Genesis Invitational
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Ludvig Aberg never felt worse leaving a golf course than three weeks ago at Torrey Pines when he shared the 36-hole lead only to get violently ill from a stomach ailment and stagger to the finish with a 79.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aggies lose heartbreaker at first place New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE (ABC4 Sports) - With first place in the Mountain West men’s basketball standings on the line, Utah State (22-4, 12-3 MW) fell just short on Sunday in Albuquerque, dropping an 82-79 thriller at The Pit. This marked the second time in two weeks the Aggies have lost to the Lobos.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump takes laps on Daytona 500 speedway in The Beast
item.description: “This is your favorite president,” Trump said. “I’m a big fan. I’m a really big fan of you people. How you do this, I don’t know. But I just want you to be safe.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Liverpool continues Premier League title charge and Man United's season keeps getting worse
item.description: Liverpool continued its charge toward the Premier League title on Sunday with a 2-1 win against Wolverhampton. For Manchester United, it just keeps getting worse after a 1-0 loss at Tottenham.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michigan State coach Tom Izzo passes Bob Knight for most Big Ten conference wins
item.description: CHAMPAIGN, Ill. (AP) — Michigan State’s Tom Izzo followed one of the most disappointing losses of his coaching career with a record-setting victory. For Illinois, it was a game in which nothing went right after halftime — or during it, thanks to an unfortunate jersey-raising snafu. The No.
item.dc:creator: STEVE STEIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NASCAR disqualifies Kligerman for too-low truck after flag-planting win at Daytona. He is appealing
item.description: Full-time television analyst and part-time NASCAR driver Parker Kligerman claimed the checkered flag for the Truck Series season opener and promptly planted it in the grass by the Daytona International Speedway logo.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Final field of 41 set for Daytona 500; Bubba Wallace, Austin Cindric win Duels
item.description: DAYTONA, Fla. (WGHP) — The field of 41 racers has officially been set for Sunday's Daytona 500. This Sunday at 1:30 p.m., the NASCAR series kicks off with the legendary Daytona 500 at Daytona International Speedway in Daytona Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WGN 9

item.title: UCSD student gets the job of a lifetime at Genesis Invitational
item.description: Ruth Nguyen, an 18-year-old student at UCSD, earned the opportunity to announce the names of golf stars at The Genesis Invitational through the TGR Foundation, founded by Tiger Woods, and its Earl Woods Scholarship program.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, Fox 5

item.title: Fraser Bullock, Brad Wilson, Steve Starks to lead organizing committee for 2034 Olympic Games
item.description: State and Olympic officials named a 25-person organizing committee, led by Committee President Fraser Bullock, former House Speaker Brad Wilson, and Utah business executive Steve Starks.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Jazz fall to Clippers in final game before All-Star break
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Norman Powell scored a season-high 41 points and James Harden added 32 to lift the Los Angeles Clippers to a 120-116 overtime victory over the Utah Jazz on Thursday night. Harden also had 10 rebounds, seven assists and three steals.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: How two women's love for the Cubs led to a life-saving kidney transplant
item.description: Cathy Weadley and Denise Vasquez met at a Cubs spring training event in 2022, where Weadley was inspired to donate her kidney to Vasquez, who was in need of a transplant.
item.dc:creator: Jewell Hillery, WGN 9

item.title: A new professional soccer team might be coming to this Southern California city
item.description: A new stadium and entertainment district fit for a professional soccer team might be coming to Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Muni will be free for NBA-All Star, Lunar New Year weekend, SFMTA announces
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- With thousands of people expected to descend on San Francisco this weekend for the NBA All-Stars and Lunar New Year Parade, officials are urging people to leave their cars at home.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: The Midwest golf company that's changing the game in Sycamore, Illinois
item.description: Sub 70, a golf company based in Sycamore, Illinois, offers custom-made golf equipment and personal service to its customers, while maintaining its Midwest roots and high-quality craftsmanship.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Jed Hoyer reacts after Alex Bregman passes over Cubs, signs with Red Sox, according to reports
item.description: NOTE: In the attached video from spring training, Cubs President Jed Hoyer discusses missing out on a bid to sign All-Star third baseman Alex Bregman in free agency. Click to watch.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: Santa Fe Christian 48, The Bishop's School 37
item.description: A big time coastal league matchup between SFC and Bishop's in boys basketball. The last time these 2 teams faced off the SFC Eagles won by just 2 points.
item.dc:creator: Allison Edmonds, Fox 5

item.title: Holmoe will be a part of search for his replacement
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After 20 years on the job, Tom Holmoe decided this past summer that this would be his last year as BYU's athletic director. "I made the decision in July of this year," Holmoe said at his retirement press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Oakland high school gym named after NBA legend, alumnus Bill Russell set to open
item.description: (KRON/BCN) -- The Oakland Unified School District will hold a ribbon-cutting ceremony on Wednesday for a new school gymnasium at McClymonds High School named after late NBA legend Bill Russell, who was an alumnus of the school. The ceremony will take place at 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4
